prompt: file_path: string, options?: { alias_resolution?: boolean, fallback_handler?: string }

Write a module that creates persistent file references (symlinks, hard links, or OS-specific shortcuts) at specified paths, handling platform differences between Windows shortcuts, macOS aliases, and Unix symlinks, with validation and error handling

A minimal utility module for modern web applications that provides two coordinated functions: (1) showModal() opens an accessible modal dialog using the native HTML dialog element and returns focus to the triggering element on close, and (2) setFocus(element) programmatically moves focus to a specified element with optional scrollIntoView. The module should handle focus tracking so that when a modal opens, the previously focused element is remembered, and when the modal closes, focus returns to that element. Include proper ARIA attributes and keyboard trap for accessibility. Target: ES6+ browsers, zero dependencies.

A React hook that accepts onSingleClick and onDoubleClick callbacks. On click, start a timer. If a second click arrives before the timer fires, treat as double-click and cancel timer. If timer fires without second click, treat as single-click. Default threshold 300ms but make it configurable.

Generate a standalone, self-contained HTML/JS/CSS mortgage payment estimator widget. It should accept three numeric inputs (principal, annual interest rate, term in years) and display the calculated monthly payment immediately as each field changes. Include optional total interest display. No external libraries or API calls—pure vanilla JS. Make it visually clean, responsive, and embeddable in other pages via a single copy-paste block.

Create a bounded resize panel component for React/Next.js applications. The component should allow users to resize a content area by dragging a handle, but enforce minimum and maximum size limits that the developer configures. Use react-resizable-panels as the underlying library, style with Tailwind CSS in the shadcn/ui fashion, write in TypeScript, and make it keyboard accessible. Include sensible default constraints but allow them to be overridden via props.

Build a lightweight, stateless month conversion utility module that transforms between month integers (1-12) and month names in full/abbreviated forms. Support locale-aware conversions for i18n applications. Must handle edge cases like case-insensitive matching, partial name matching, and invalid input validation. Output as an ES6 module with TypeScript definitions for direct import into forms, reporting engines, or data normalization pipelines.
sourceAudioFiles: array of audio files in common formats (WAV, AIFF, MP3, OGG, FLAC), targetFormat: { codec: string, sampleRate: number, bitDepth: number, channels: number }, batchMode: boolean
A reusable automation script template with header metadata (name, version, description, dependencies, installation steps) and delimiter-separated script body for injection into target projects

I need a standalone calculator that takes a 2x2 contingency table (a,b,c,d) where a=both raters agree yes, b=rater A yes/rater B no, c=rater A no/rater B yes, d=both raters agree no. Calculate Cohen's Kappa = (observed agreement - chance agreement) / (1 - chance agreement) where observed agreement = (a+d)/total and chance agreement = ((a+b)(a+c) + (c+d)(b+d)) / total^2. Display the Kappa value rounded to 3 decimals and provide a text interpretation using the standard scale: <0 = poor, 0.01-0.20 = slight, 0.21-0.40 = fair, 0.41-0.60 = moderate, 0.61-0.80 = substantial, 0.81-1.00 = almost perfect. Include a brief note showing the formula or the calculation steps. No data storage, no external API calls, no login. Just input cells, calculate button, and result.
A React hook that tracks mouse cursor position and returns both viewport-relative coordinates and element-relative coordinates when hovering a target ref
Create a command palette interface with grouped commands, keyboard-driven navigation, and contextual tooltips
A lightweight floating button palette for desktop automation that sends configurable commands to a target application via IPC, local scripting, or network relay
installed font proof sheet generator local system fonts batch print PDF export

itemList:array, initialSelection:string|number|null, anchorElement:DOMRect|coordinates, placement:auto|bottom|top|start|end, menuMaxHeight:number
Build pipeline environment, target binary path(s), version metadata map (version string, file version, product version, company name, product name, copyright, description)

a module that normalizes freeform date strings into ISO 8601 or locale-specific canonical format, handling natural language inputs like 'next friday', '2 weeks ago', 'tomorrow', as well as common formats like MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, and returning structured output with parsed date or specific error codes
source text, source character(s), target character(s), optional: transformation rules file
ZIP code string (5-digit or 9-digit ZIP+4)

Create a bounded audio utilities module that provides: (1) Audio recording functions with configurable options (sample rate, format, quality), (2) Audio playback controls (play, pause, stop, seek, volume), (3) Audio file management for saving/loading recordings, (4) Audio session configuration for platform-specific behavior including background playback support, (5) Demo examples showing integration patterns. The module should be suitable for embedding in larger applications and follow a utility/library pattern rather than a standalone app. Include clear APIs for initialization, recording lifecycle management, playback state handling, and resource cleanup.

Create a bounded module that allows users to apply a property change to multiple selected UI elements at once. The module should: accept a selection of items; compute which properties are common to all selected items; enable/disable bulk actions based on common properties; apply a property change (like enable/disable, show/hide, set value) to all selected items in one operation.

Given the retained row, derive one specific, bounded prompt. The prompt should be a realistic user request for an AI coding assistant, framed in present-day terms, asking for a small utility that fulfills the coreUserJob. The user should describe what they're building and what specific help they need.
rootFolderPath: string, searchPattern: string, options?: { recursive?: boolean, caseSensitive?: boolean, maxDepth?: number }

A user wants to be alerted when new items arrive in a shared mailbox, intake folder, or monitored queue without having to open the main application. They need configurable alert intervals, a system tray indicator showing unread count, and a way to mark items as seen. The module runs locally as a background service and watches an IMAP account, local folder, or API endpoint.
input dataset: array of objects; requested fields: string[]; options: { renameMap?, missingFieldStrategy?, preserveTypes? }

Build a reusable hierarchical select component for nested option selection with cascading submenus, supporting multiple levels, terminal selection actions, WAI-ARIA accessibility, and keyboard navigation
tooltip component library for web and desktop applications with image support
User selects files/folders/applications to include in their startup workspace. User specifies the target workspace path and launch order. User enables/disables the startup bootstrap.
I need a quick way to launch my most-used apps AND keep a small encrypted stash of private items (passwords, API keys, notes) in one local command bar that lives on my machine.
Resolve the application path for bundle identifier: com.apple.Safari

Create a JavaScript/TypeScript npm module that converts numbers between standard decimal notation and scientific (exponential) notation while preserving significant figures and trailing zeros that indicate precision. The module should export functions: toScientific(number: number | string, sigFigs: number): string and toDecimal(number: number | string): string. Must handle edge cases like 1.00 (three sig figs), 0.0010 (two sig figs), large numbers like 1.5e10, and ensure trailing zeros are not stripped when they represent significant precision. Include TypeScript types and tests.

I need to repair a broken application script/config without launching the application because it's in a crashed/broken state. The app won't open normally, so I need an external tool that can read and edit its source code, automation scripts, or configuration files directly on disk without executing the target runtime. I want to inspect what scripts exist, make targeted repairs, and save changes atomically without triggering the broken execution path.

Create a single-screen demo module that displays 4-6 interactive buttons showcasing different microinteraction states: hover (scale/color shift), focus (ring/indicator), active/press (depth/scale down), disabled (muted opacity), and success-state transition. Each button should be clearly labeled with its interaction type. Use vanilla JavaScript and CSS with CSS custom properties for theme values. Include a simple panel showing current active state for debugging/demo purposes.

Create a bounded software component that renders TIFF images within a host application window, supporting 1/2/4/8/24-bit depths and common compressions, with adaptive palette generation for constrained displays, suitable for embedding in document or asset management workflows
A numeric value either in 'XpY' format (X picas, Y points) or as integer/float of total points
build a user inactivity detector module that tracks time since last user interaction and triggers callbacks when idle threshold is exceeded

Create a minimal C-to-[HOST] plugin wrapper template that takes an existing C function and exposes it as a callable extension in [HOST], handling parameter passing, return values, and memory management automatically. Include: (1) Wrapper glue code template, (2) Build configuration for [TARGET_TOOLCHAIN], (3) Example of wrapping a sample C function, (4) Documentation mapping common C types to [HOST] types with notes on string/handle/pointer handling.

Create a bounded module that enables users to paste an image from their clipboard directly onto a canvas at specific x/y coordinates. The module should capture clipboard content on paste action (Ctrl+V), parse image data, provide coordinate input fields or click-to-place functionality, render the image using canvas drawImage method at the specified position, and include bounds checking to ensure the image places within canvas limits.
directoryPath: string, filterByType?: string[], filterByCreator?: string, includeSubfolders?: boolean, returnFormat?: 'names' | 'paths' | 'fullMetadata'
Write a function that converts a positive number into its written English word equivalent (e.g., 1234 → 'one thousand two hundred thirty-four'). Support optional currency handling for USD with cents.
React component that disables/block specific keyboard shortcuts (cmd+shift, option+click, etc.) to prevent unauthorized access to hidden admin controls or edit modes in a guided app interface
Input: Selection range object, style configuration object, mode flag. Output: Wrapped span element with applied styles.

Write a simple utility module that provides three functions: read_clipboard() returns the current system clipboard contents, write_clipboard(data) places data onto the system clipboard, and clipboard_info() returns metadata about what's currently on the clipboard (format, size, whether it's empty). The module should be self-contained, minimal, and work on the user's operating system without external dependencies beyond standard libraries.
a developer wants to add common utility functions to their app without writing each one from scratch - needs dialogs, file helpers, progress indicators, UI controls, audio, system utilities
An app needs to display its current version, build number, and optionally deployment ring or environment to users or logs. The metadata is embedded at build time.
script bundle(s) to optimize, target output path, optimization level

Analyze the structure of my project at path [PROJECT_PATH]. I want to inspect [OBJECT_CATEGORIES] (e.g., classes, functions, modules, interfaces, fields). Generate a report at [DETAIL_LEVEL] (summary/deep) and export to text file.
I need a function that converts a number from one base to another. Input: '1010' from base 2 to base 10 should return '10'. Input: 'FF' from base 16 to base 2 should return '11111111'.

Create a local-first launcher module with a command palette interface. It should: 1) Scan specified directories to discover available apps, workspaces, and documents; 2) Build an index of existing targets only; 3) Present a searchable dropdown menu; 4) Allow manual refresh to update the index; 5) Open the selected target with the system default handler. Keep it sovereign and local - no cloud dependencies.
none required - self-executing utility
module-name, module-description, user-journeys-array, api-surface-summary

a string of multiline text, a prefix character or string, and an integer repeat count

A micro-module that detects keyboard modifier chords (e.g., Cmd+Option) and routes them through hierarchical event scopes. Must support: 1) local component-level shortcut handlers that intercept first, 2) parent app-level handlers as fallback, 3) proper event propagation with stopPropagation, 4) registration API for scoped shortcuts. The module exports: registerShortcut(scope, chord, handler), unregisterShortcut(scope, chord), onKeyChord(callback). Scope hierarchy: component > container > app. When a chord is pressed, check local scope first, then bubble to parent scope until handler returns true (handled) or reaches top.
Build a bounded developer tool module for scoped script search and snippet management focused on UI component scripts.
The directory path to scan, optional file extension whitelist, desired output file path, merge order (alphabetical, depth-first, modified-date), and optional delimiter between merged files.
launch external media file with chrome hidden, restore chrome on close

Create a VS Code extension that adds an Object Editor palette for visually creating UI components, includes BaseConvert and character utility functions, and registers new commands in the Command Palette for rapid component scaffolding
A bounded prompt draft for building this module
user wants to quickly create, duplicate, delete pages and insert UI components (buttons, fields) without leaving their editor
A user wants to work with multiple content areas in one view, each scrolling independently

Create a TypeScript module that detects whether Caps Lock (and optionally Num Lock / Scroll Lock) is currently active. Export a simple API: isModifierActive(modifier: 'capslock' | 'numlock' | 'scrolllock'): boolean. Include a helper for password-field use-cases: checkCapsLockOnFocus() that returns caps-lock state when a password input gains focus. Keep it lightweight and dependency-free where possible, using the KeyboardEvent.getModifierState() API for browser contexts and a small native addon or platform-specific fallback for Node/Electron environments if write-access is needed.

Create a lightweight, local-first XOR text encryption/decryption module. This is a bounded utility for obscuring short text blocks with a shared key. Feature set: (1) plaintext textarea for source text, (2) key input field, (3) 'Encrypt' and 'Decrypt' buttons, (4) result textarea with copy-to-clipboard, (5) all processing happens in-memory only. Implementation: XOR each byte with repeating key, then transform result bytes into printable ASCII range 33-126 for safe transmission. Decryption reverses this. The UX should be minimal, focused, and fast—open, paste, type key, encrypt/decrypt, copy result. No cloud services, no accounts, no tracking. Single HTML file with embedded CSS/JS is appropriate.

Build a minimal bounded module for runtime version detection. It should query the current runtime environment (Node.js process.versions, OS version, or similar) and return the version as a structured object. Include a simple compatibility checker that takes a minimum version and returns a boolean. The module should have zero external dependencies and be suitable for use as a local-first utility in feature gating or diagnostic workflows.

Create a lightweight, zero-dependency npm package called "tiny-ui-helpers" containing ES6 modules for common UI utility functions: dialog helpers for confirmations and quit prompts, component/field toggle functions for visibility and state changes, timestamp utilities for inserting/formatted dates, and quick action handlers for buttons. Export each as tree-shakeable functions. Include TypeScript types. Target: frontend devs needing quick UI helpers without a framework.

Build a bounded embeddable help widget SDK that renders contextual help content within a host application, supports programmatic triggers via JavaScript/Native API to show specific help topics based on application state, includes local content storage with markdown/JSON support, provides navigation controls (back, toc, search), and emits events for analytics on help usage.
One or more reactant formulas with masses in grams or millimoles, a product formula with its actual mass obtained, and an output format preference (ACS or RSC style)

Build a bounded module that serves as a sandbox for safely creating and customizing floating tool panels (palettes) in desktop applications. The module should allow users to configure panel attributes like position, size, behavior, and styling without risking breakage of the host application. Include live preview, attribute editing, safety validation, and export functionality. Use modern UI frameworks like floating-ui or similar for positioning logic. The module should feel like a contained tool palette builder rather than a full IDE.

Create a bounded text case transformation utility module that provides functions to convert strings to uppercase, lowercase, title case, and initial capitals. Include batch processing for arrays of strings or record fields. Support locale-aware case handling for languages like Turkish. The module should be a single-purpose helper suitable for inclusion in automation scripts or form-processing pipelines.
searchPattern, sourceData, delimiter, searchMode
> user wants to reset their editor panels to a saved layout. they want named panels (terminal, explorer, debug, output) to snap to their predefined positions with one command.
List all available CLI tools from PATH and node_modules/.bin with fuzzy search and one-command launch

Build a bounded drag-to-select component that: tracks pointer-down/move/up events within a container; renders a semi-transparent rectangle with dashed border during the drag; computes normalized coordinates handling drags in any direction; returns {x, y, width, height} to the consumer on release. No external dependencies. Vanilla JS/TS preferred for broad reusability.

Create a bounded module representing an embedded command palette that provides utility commands for clipboard manipulation, text search/replace (grep), hex dump of binary data, file save/export, and system queries. The module should include a command library with descriptions and examples, bound actions for clipboard content and current document, and export operations. This mirrors HyperCard's Isaac stack which provided 88 commands via XCMD/XFCN for in-stack automation without leaving the workspace.
targetApp, commandList, fileSet, options
tabular data array (rows of fields), column configuration specifying which columns must be numeric

You are building a VS Code extension that provides mathematical utilities via the command palette and exported API. Include: (1) Command palette entries for evaluating math expressions, base conversion, and complex number operations. (2) Exported functions module exposing add, multiply, divide, subtract, power, log10, logBase, baseConvert, complexAdd, complexMultiply, complexDivide. (3) Use Math.js for expression parsing. (4) Register commands in package.json under contributions.commands. (5) Export the utility API for other extensions to consume via extension.exports.
Grateful Dead concert archive setlist venue date

You are a bounded input normalization module. Your job is to take freeform user-entered phone number strings and normalize them to a clean numeric format by stripping all non-numeric characters. Apply these rules: (1) Remove all characters except digits 0-9. (2) Detect if the resulting digit string is 7, 10, or 11 digits. (3) Reject or flag strings that do not match these lengths. (4) Return the normalized numeric string for valid inputs. Do not attempt full phone validation, international formatting, or carrier/geocoder lookups. Keep it lightweight and focused on normalization only.
sourceRecordId, targetTemplateId, options? (preserveCustomLogic?, validateCompatibility?)

Create a local-first Regex Text Transformer module with these characteristics: (1) accepts raw text input via paste or file load, (2) provides regex pattern input with validation feedback, (3) supports replacement templates with $1-$N backreference syntax, (4) shows match count and highlights all matches in the source text, (5) displays live preview of transformed output, (6) supports global replace-all operation, (7) processes entirely client-side with no server uploads, (8) exports result as text file. The module is a focused utility for data cleanup and text transformation workflows, not a general text editor.

View large text document with fast scrolling

Create a bounded JavaScript module that renders charts from structured data input. The module should accept JSON data and chart configuration, render visualizations using canvas or SVG, support multiple chart types (bar, line, pie, scatter), and provide export capabilities. Prioritize local-first operation, zero external runtime dependencies, and embeddability within other applications. Include type definitions and a clean public API.

Create a bounded module that enables a host application to embed and run an external component as a self-contained window or panel. The module should handle loading the component bundle, instantiating it in an isolated runtime, rendering it inside the host's UI hierarchy, and establishing bidirectional communication for data exchange and lifecycle events.
Place, scale, import, and capture graphics for use in visual layouts and printed reports

A PowerShell user needs to perform file operations, path parsing, system queries, or text formatting tasks in their automation scripts but finds the built-in cmdlets cumbersome or incomplete for these specific utility actions.
targetVolumeURI, credentials, protocolPreference, mountOptions

A bounded clipboard utility module that copies text to the system clipboard from app state and pastes text from the system clipboard into app variables. Must work cross-browser without Flash or frameworks. Include demo workflows for importing text files and auditing clipboard contents.

Create a bounded contextual tooltip module that displays inline help balloons anchored to UI elements. The module should support: 1) registering tooltip content for target elements, 2) positioning tooltips relative to anchors with configurable placement (top/bottom/left/right), 3) controlling tip/arrow position where it joins the balloon, 4) a global toggle to enable/disable all inline help, 5) programmatic API to check if help is active, and 6) collision detection to keep tooltips on-screen. This is a focused component, not a full help system.

project source directory or template bundle with mixed debug/production code and UI elements

Create a local-first embedded script extraction tool that reads Office documents (Word, Excel with VBA macros), compiled assemblies (DLL/EXE with embedded resources), and binary formats containing embedded scripts. Export all extracted scripts to readable text files organized by source. Support batch extraction from directories. Output includes source filename, script type, and extracted code. The tool runs offline with no network dependency.
targetDate, referenceDate, returnFormat

Create a bounded module for managing z-index/layer ordering of canvas elements. The module should provide: 1) Bring to Front and Send to Back actions for moving elements to extremes, 2) Raise and Lower actions for single-step movement, 3) A layer panel showing current stacking order, 4) Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+] to raise, Ctrl+[ to lower, Ctrl+Shift+] to front, Ctrl+Shift+[ to back), 5) Undo/redo support for layer changes, 6) Visual feedback showing element position in stack. Elements can be UI components, shapes, or canvas objects. The module should be selection-aware and update the layer panel when selection changes.

Create a local-first script launcher and utility pack for power users to run small reusable automation scripts. Include categorized scripts for common developer tasks like file operations, text transformations, system automation, and project scaffolding. Provide hotkey binding support, a quick-access launcher UI, and a lightweight plugin system for extending the script library.

A React component that renders a custom-styled dropdown select with configurable typography, sizing, and border treatment. The component accepts an options array, selected value, and onChange callback. It supports custom theming via CSS variables or styled-components tokens. Includes a cache hook to preload and store menu options in memory for faster subsequent renders. The trigger button displays the selected value and opens the dropdown on click. The dropdown panel displays options with hover states and keyboard navigation support.
Select source files, choose copyright template, confirm apply, review audit log

Create a bounded music notation editor module that: 1) Provides a text editor for composing music in LilyPond-style notation or similar scriptable format; 2) Supports save/open of project files to local storage; 3) Includes audio preview/playback of the notation; 4) Enables copying the notation output to clipboard for pasting into external script-based workflows (like HyperCard scripts, Python music libraries, or DAW automation); 5) Works entirely local-first with no cloud dependencies. The module should be a single sovereign tool, not part of a broader suite.
target_menu: str, target_item: str | None = None

Write a small, focused utility that calculates the day of year (1-366) for any date and optionally calculates days remaining in the year. Support output formats: raw integer, string like 'Day 198 (167 remaining)', tuple like [198, 167], or just the ordinal number.

Load a target Chrome extension in instrumented mode, capture all API calls it makes to the Chrome extension runtime, display call trace with parameters and context in a developer panel, allow selection of any captured call to set a custom return value or conditional override, persist interception rules for reuse in test scenarios
context: dragState, pointerPosition, viewportBounds, contentBounds, elapsedTime

Implement a JavaScript module that provides audio playback control with the following features: transport actions (play, pause, stop, eject, seek forward/backward), time queries (elapsed time, remaining time, duration), track metadata access (track count, current track index, track title/artist from ID3, track length), and track range selection (from track index to track index). The module should be stateful, expose current playback state, and handle async metadata loading. Use HTML5 Audio API.

Create a bounded local-first module containing a catalog of reusable vanilla JavaScript and CSS interaction patterns for common UI behaviors including drag-and-drop, text selection, keyboard shortcuts, double-click detection, auto-scrolling fields, button animations, pop-ups, and simple file read/write operations. Each pattern should be a self-contained, copy-pasteable example demonstrating one specific interaction technique.
current machine network identity metadata

Create an ACP module for Plaited as a self-contained module rather than a root runtime script. The module should provide a privileged internal control surface for the top-level agent only, use a remote-capable local transport such as a Unix socket as the primary connection type, allow SSH-tunneled access to an already running Plaited instance, and treat stdio only as an optional adapter rather than the main model. Resolve the top-level agent through bootstrap-owned wiring instead of direct loop construction, and keep the ACP surface separate from A2A and browser WebSocket transport. Keep the implementation local-first, bounded, and explicit about its control-boundary role.
